---
title: "Auto Loan Credit"
subtitle: "ABS-EE Loan-Level Panel — SEC EDGAR"
description: "Live delinquency trends from SEC EDGAR ABS-EE filings. Loan-level auto credit data, parsed from servicer reports so you don't have to."
toc: true
freeze: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="data-note">
  <strong>Data source:</strong> SEC EDGAR ABS-EE autoloan panel —
  loan-level data from asset-backed securities filings.
  Parsed from raw XML filings via the bens-data-lake pipeline.
  Coverage limited to SEC-registered auto ABS trusts.
  See <a href="../data-sources.qmd">Data Sources</a> for full methodology.
</div>
```

This is structured finance data — the kind of thing you'd normally pay a Bloomberg terminal
to look at, except it's sitting in SEC EDGAR and nobody told you.
ABS-EE filings contain loan-level performance data for auto loan ABS trusts:
origination date, balance, days past due, payment status. The good stuff.

Coverage varies by reporting month as trusts enter and exit the panel.
A delinquency rate computed from 2M loans means something very different from one computed from 200K.
Check the volume chart.

---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

import { DuckDBClient } from "npm:@duckdb/duckdb-wasm"
db = DuckDBClient.of({})
r2Base = params["r2-base-url"]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// Load all three DPD buckets in one query — controls filter client-side
panelData = {
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") return null
  try {
    const result = await db.query(`
      SELECT
        filing_date,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE days_past_due >= 30) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS d30,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE days_past_due >= 60) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS d60,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE days_past_due >= 90) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS d90,
        COUNT(*) AS total_loans
      FROM read_parquet('${r2Base}/abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/**/*.parquet', union_by_name=true)
      WHERE filing_date IS NOT NULL
      GROUP BY filing_date
      ORDER BY filing_date
    `)
    return Array.from(result).map(d => ({
      ...d,
      filing_date: new Date(d.filing_date),
      d30: Number(d.d30),
      d60: Number(d.d60),
      d90: Number(d.d90),
      total_loans: Number(d.total_loans)
    }))
  } catch(e) {
    return { error: e.message }
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

NBER_ABS = [
  { start: new Date("2007-12-01"), end: new Date("2009-06-01") },
  { start: new Date("2020-02-01"), end: new Date("2020-04-01") }
]

DPD_META = ({
  30: { col: "d30", label: "30+ DPD", color: "#C8A058" },
  60: { col: "d60", label: "60+ DPD", color: "#B07040" },
  90: { col: "d90", label: "90+ DPD", color: "#4A6FA5" }
})
```

## Latest Month

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>◌</span><span>Live data — chart loads when the data lake is ready.</span></div>`
  }
  if (!panelData || panelData.error) {
    return html`<div class="chart-error"><span>⚠</span><span>Data unavailable — ${panelData?.error ?? "check R2 connection"}.</span></div>`
  }

  const latest = panelData[panelData.length - 1]
  const prior  = panelData[panelData.length - 2]
  const yearAgo = panelData.find(d => {
    const tgt = new Date(latest.filing_date)
    tgt.setFullYear(tgt.getFullYear() - 1)
    return Math.abs(d.filing_date - tgt) < 45 * 86400000
  })

  const fmt = v => v != null ? v.toFixed(2) + "%" : "—"
  const fmtDelta = (a, b, col) => {
    if (a == null || b == null) return ""
    const delta = a[col] - b[col]
    const sign = delta > 0 ? "+" : ""
    const color = delta > 0 ? "#B04A38" : "#3A7A5A"
    const arrow = delta > 0 ? "▲" : "▼"
    return `<div style="font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:0.63rem;margin-top:0.3rem;color:${color}">${arrow} ${sign}${delta.toFixed(2)}pp vs prior mo</div>`
  }
  const fmtYoY = (a, b, col) => {
    if (a == null || b == null) return ""
    const delta = a[col] - b[col]
    const sign = delta > 0 ? "+" : ""
    const color = delta > 0 ? "#B04A38" : "#3A7A5A"
    return `<div style="font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:0.63rem;margin-top:0.15rem;color:${color}">${sign}${delta.toFixed(2)}pp YoY</div>`
  }

  const latestDateStr = latest.filing_date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "long", year: "numeric" })
  const loans = latest.total_loans >= 1e6
    ? `${(latest.total_loans / 1e6).toFixed(2)}M`
    : `${(latest.total_loans / 1e3).toFixed(0)}K`

  return html`<div>
    <div style="font-family:'DM Mono',monospace;font-size:0.68rem;letter-spacing:0.06em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#8A8A8A;margin-bottom:0.5rem">Latest filing: ${latestDateStr}</div>
    <div class="kpi-strip">
      <div class="kpi-card">
        <div class="kpi-value" style="color:#4A6FA5">${fmt(latest.d90)}</div>
        <div class="kpi-label">90+ Day Delinquency</div>
        ${fmtDelta(latest, prior, "d90")}
        ${fmtYoY(latest, yearAgo, "d90")}
      </div>
      <div class="kpi-card">
        <div class="kpi-value" style="color:#C8A058">${fmt(latest.d30)}</div>
        <div class="kpi-label">30+ Day Delinquency</div>
        ${fmtDelta(latest, prior, "d30")}
        ${fmtYoY(latest, yearAgo, "d30")}
      </div>
      <div class="kpi-card">
        <div class="kpi-value">${loans}</div>
        <div class="kpi-label">Loans in Panel</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>`
}
```

## Delinquency Trends

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof dpdBucket = Inputs.radio(
  [30, 60, 90],
  {
    value: 90,
    label: "DPD bucket",
    format: v => `${v}+ days`
  }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof timeWindow = Inputs.radio(
  ["1Y", "2Y", "3Y", "All"],
  { value: "All", label: "Period" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

filteredPanel = {
  if (!panelData || panelData.error) return []
  const cutoff = (() => {
    if (timeWindow === "All") return new Date(0)
    const d = new Date()
    d.setFullYear(d.getFullYear() - parseInt(timeWindow))
    return d
  })()
  return panelData.filter(d => d.filing_date >= cutoff)
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!r2Base || r2Base === "") {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>◌</span><span>Live data — chart loads when the data lake is ready.</span></div>`
  }
  if (!panelData || panelData.error) {
    return html`<div class="chart-error"><span>⚠</span><span>Data unavailable — ${panelData?.error ?? "unknown error"}.</span></div>`
  }

  const meta = DPD_META[dpdBucket]
  const maxDate = d3.max(filteredPanel, d => d.filing_date)
  const asOfStr = maxDate
    ? maxDate.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "numeric" })
    : "unknown"

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 820,
    height: 360,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginRight: 20,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {
      type: "time",
      label: null,
      tickFormat: d => d.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "2-digit" })
    },
    y: {
      label: `Delinquency Rate — ${meta.label} (%)`,
      tickFormat: d => d.toFixed(1) + "%",
      grid: true,
      gridColor: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)",
      zero: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.rect(NBER_ABS, {
        x1: "start",
        x2: "end",
        fill: "#8A8A8A",
        fillOpacity: 0.09
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)" }),
      Plot.areaY(filteredPanel, {
        x: "filing_date",
        y: meta.col,
        fill: meta.color,
        fillOpacity: 0.12
      }),
      Plot.line(filteredPanel, {
        x: "filing_date",
        y: meta.col,
        stroke: meta.color,
        strokeWidth: 2,
        curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(filteredPanel, Plot.selectLast({
        x: "filing_date",
        y: meta.col,
        fill: meta.color,
        r: 5
      })),
      Plot.tip(filteredPanel, Plot.pointerX({
        x: "filing_date",
        y: meta.col,
        title: d => `${d.filing_date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", {month:"short",year:"numeric"})}\n${meta.label}: ${d[meta.col].toFixed(2)}%\nLoans: ${(d.total_loans/1e6).toFixed(2)}M`
      }))
    ],
    caption: `Source: SEC EDGAR ABS-EE | ${meta.label} rate | Shaded = NBER recessions | As of ${asOfStr}`
  })
}
```

## All Three Buckets

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!panelData || panelData.error || !r2Base) return null

  const cutoff = (() => {
    if (timeWindow === "All") return new Date(0)
    const d = new Date()
    d.setFullYear(d.getFullYear() - parseInt(timeWindow))
    return d
  })()

  // Reshape to long form for multi-line chart
  const long = []
  for (const row of filteredPanel) {
    for (const [bucket, meta] of Object.entries(DPD_META)) {
      long.push({ filing_date: row.filing_date, bucket: meta.label, rate: row[meta.col] })
    }
  }

  const BUCKET_COLORS = {
    "30+ DPD": "#C8A058",
    "60+ DPD": "#B07040",
    "90+ DPD": "#4A6FA5"
  }
  const BUCKET_ORDER = ["30+ DPD", "60+ DPD", "90+ DPD"]

  return Plot.plot({
    title: "All Delinquency Buckets",
    subtitle: "30+, 60+, and 90+ day past due — same panel, same period",
    width: 820,
    height: 300,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginRight: 20,
    marginBottom: 50,
    color: {
      domain: BUCKET_ORDER,
      range: BUCKET_ORDER.map(b => BUCKET_COLORS[b]),
      legend: true
    },
    x: {
      type: "time",
      label: null,
      tickFormat: d => d.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "2-digit" })
    },
    y: {
      label: "Delinquency Rate (%)",
      tickFormat: d => d.toFixed(1) + "%",
      grid: true,
      gridColor: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)"
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.rect(NBER_ABS, { x1: "start", x2: "end", fill: "#8A8A8A", fillOpacity: 0.09 }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)" }),
      Plot.line(long, {
        x: "filing_date",
        y: "rate",
        stroke: "bucket",
        strokeWidth: 1.8,
        curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.tip(long, Plot.pointerX({
        x: "filing_date",
        y: "rate",
        stroke: "bucket"
      }))
    ],
    caption: "Source: SEC EDGAR ABS-EE | All buckets are inclusive (30+ includes 60+ and 90+)"
  })
}
```

## Loan Volume

Total loans in the panel by filing month. More loans = better sample.
Drops in coverage mean trusts exited the filing window — not that loans were paid off.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  if (!panelData || panelData.error || !r2Base) {
    return html`<div class="chart-loading"><span>◌</span><span>Waiting for data...</span></div>`
  }

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 820,
    height: 240,
    marginLeft: 65,
    marginRight: 20,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {
      type: "time",
      label: null,
      tickFormat: d => d.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "2-digit" })
    },
    y: {
      label: "Loans in Panel",
      tickFormat: d => d >= 1e6 ? `${(d/1e6).toFixed(1)}M` : d >= 1e3 ? `${(d/1e3).toFixed(0)}K` : d,
      grid: true,
      gridColor: "var(--border-color, #D8D3C8)"
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.rect(NBER_ABS, { x1: "start", x2: "end", fill: "#8A8A8A", fillOpacity: 0.09 }),
      Plot.areaY(filteredPanel, {
        x: "filing_date",
        y: "total_loans",
        fill: "#4A6FA5",
        fillOpacity: 0.15
      }),
      Plot.line(filteredPanel, {
        x: "filing_date",
        y: "total_loans",
        stroke: "#4A6FA5",
        strokeWidth: 1.5,
        curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.tip(filteredPanel, Plot.pointerX({
        x: "filing_date",
        y: "total_loans",
        title: d => `${d.filing_date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", {month:"short",year:"numeric"})}\nLoans: ${(d.total_loans/1e6).toFixed(2)}M`
      }))
    ],
    caption: "Source: SEC EDGAR ABS-EE | Drops indicate trusts exiting filing window, not loan payoffs"
  })
}
```

---

## Methodology

**What counts as delinquent**: A loan is `days_past_due >= X` at the servicer-reported filing date.
The 90+ bucket is the most severe and the most commonly cited. The 30+ bucket picks up stress earlier
but is noisier — minor payment timing issues show up here that never progress.

**What's included**: All loans in Hive-partitioned Parquet files under
`abs_ee/curated/asset_class=autoloan/` on R2. Coverage limited to issuers registered
with the SEC under Regulation AB II.

**Known limitations**: Servicer definitions of "days past due" vary by issuer. A 30 DPD loan
at Ally may not be identical to a 30 DPD loan at Santander. This is the data as filed —
no cross-issuer harmonization has been applied.

**Recession shading:** NBER-defined recession dates. EDGAR ABS-EE coverage begins November 2016,
so only the COVID recession (Feb–Apr 2020) falls within the panel.

For full source documentation, see [Data Sources →](../data-sources.qmd)
